articles.title: LNG: Oil shock boosts fuel-switch economics – ING
articles.expand: ING economists Gerben Hieminga and Rico Luman argue that the Middle East conflict and closure of the Strait of Hormuz have sharply lifted oil-based bunker costs, improving LNG’s relative appeal.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: BoC minutes watched for policy clues – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team highlights that markets will scrutinize the Bank of Canada’s Summary of Deliberations for more detail on April’s decision and two‑sided guidance.

articles.title: ECB’s Rehn: Monetary policy should not be based on oil prices alone
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) official and Finnish Central Bank Governor Olli Rehn said during European trading hours on Wednesday that higher oil prices should not be the only factor to direct monetary policy decisions. Rehn added that the central bank needs to assess the scope of energy shocks.

articles.title: ECB to hike interest rates in June – Reuters poll
articles.expand: According to a Reuters poll, the European Central Bank (ECB) will hike the deposit rate by 25 basis points (bps) to 2.25% in June, said 59 of 70 economists (vs 44 of 85 in April survey).

articles.title: The Euro and Sterling are Threatening to Break Lower, while a Record Current Account Surplus Does not Prevent the Yen from Challenging Intervention Levels
articles.expand: The lack of progress in re-opening the Strait of Hormuz has not prevented oil prices from stabilizing or risk-appetites improving today. Equities and bonds are trading with a firmer bias. The Trump-Xi meeting tomorrow is key talking point today. Ahead of it, the PBOC set the dollar’s reference rate at a new three-year low. US Treasury Secretary Bes...

articles.title: ECB's Muller: Fast Hormuz solution needed to hold in June
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker Madis Muller said on Wednesday that a fast resolution in the Strait of hormuz is needed for the ECB to hold rates unchanged in June.

articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: US Dollar Rises Against Loonie - 13 May 2026
articles.expand: USD/CAD is testing the 50-day EMA as dollar momentum builds, with 1.3750 the key breakout level and 1.36 as support if the move fades.

articles.title: US Dollar: Hot CPI keeps Fed cautious – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team highlights that United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) data showed strong core and services inflation, reinforcing concerns about persistent underlying price pressures. Shelter and super-core components rose, while core goods remained flat.

articles.title: USD/CHF Forecast: Can Rising US Yields Drive the Dollar Back Toward 0.80? - 13 May 2026
articles.expand: USD/CHF remains bullish as rising US rates and a wide yield differential favor the dollar, with 0.79 and 0.80 as the next upside targets.

articles.title: ECB's Muller: The ECB will need a fast resolution on Hormuz to hold in June
articles.expand: Eurozone hasn't fallen into stagflation Don't see reasons to talk about recession now The ECB will need a fast resolution on Hormuz to hold in June While the US-Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz have pushed headline inflation higher and dampened economic activity, Muller remains cautiously optimistic about the underlying economic fou...

articles.title: Indian Rupee struggles for relief despite centre hikes import duty on Gold and Silver
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) strives to regain ground against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday. The USD/INR pair is almost flat, close to its all-time high of 95.70, even as the increase in import duty on Gold and Silver to 15% from 6% by the Indian government.

articles.title: Australian Dollar outperforms its peers as hawkish RBA bets swell further
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades higher against its major currency peers, flattening against the US Dollar (USD) around 0.7240, during the European trading session on Wednesday.

articles.title: Eurozone GDP Expands Just 0.1% in Q1, Annual Growth Slows to 0.8%
articles.expand: Eurozone economic growth slowed further in the first quarter of 2026 as higher energy costs, weak industrial activity, and lingering geopolitical uncertainty continued weighing on momentum. According to Eurostat’s flash estimate, seasonally adjusted GDP rose just 0.1% qoq in the Eurozone, down from 0.2% growth in Q4 of 2025. Across the broader Euro...

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Bears taking control with 0.5930 support under pressure
articles.expand: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) is showing the weakest performance of the G8 currencies on Wednesday, heading lower for the second consecutive day against a stronger US Dollar (USD), with NZD/USD bears testing the bottom of the weekly range at 0.5930 at the time of writing.

articles.title: Oil: Rising prices with Middle East risk – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes that concerns over the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz have pushed Oil prices higher, with Dated Brent moving above $111.

articles.expand: Prior +0.3%; revised to +0.2% There was a negative revision to the Q4 estimate, and that's the only minor tweak to the preliminary report. Other than that, this just reaffirms that the euro area economy did post marginal growth in the first quarter of the year. articles.title: IEA says world oil supply to fall by 3.9 million bpd in 2026
articles.expand: According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), the world oil supply to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) in 2026 assuming the oil flows from the Strait of Hormuz, a critical passage to almost 20% of global energy supply, will gradually resume from June (prev. forecast 1.5 million bpd fa

articles.title: Why April’s U.S. CPI Report Put Stagflation Back on the Map
articles.expand: The April CPI just confirmed what many feared: the U.S. is inside a stagflation scenario, and the Fed has no clean way out.

articles.title: USD/JPY Analysis for May 13, 2026: Bulls Test Resistance as Intervention Risk Lingers
articles.expand: With the Fed frozen by inflation and the BOJ getting serious about rate hikes, USD/JPY is caught in the middle of a policy tug-of-war.

articles.title: S&P 500: Equities pressured by Oil and inflation – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid notes that higher Oil prices and hawkish US inflation data created a challenging backdrop for equities.

articles.title: WTI Price Forecast: Struggles to reclaim $100, outlook remains firm
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, corrects to near $97.20 during the European trading session on Wednesday. The Oil price gives back some of its recent gains as escalating hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) bets have raised concerns over the oil demand outlook.

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Hawkish repricing supports DXY – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC’s Christopher Wong notes the US Dollar (USD) strengthened after hotter United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) data lifted UST yields and revived Fed hike expectations, with focus now on US Producer Price Index (PPI).

articles.expand: New Zealand’s latest RBNZ Survey of Expectations showed a sharp rise in near-term inflation expectations, reinforcing concerns that price pressures may remain elevated even as growth slows. articles.title: OECD Sees BoJ Raising Rates to 2% by End-2027
articles.expand: The OECD projected on Wednesday that the Bank of Japan will continue steadily normalizing monetary policy, with the short-term policy rate expected to rise from the current 0.75% to 2% by the end of 2027. articles.expand: The OECD projects the Bank of Japan will raise its policy rate to 2% by end-2027 from 0.75% currently, backing continued hikes on solid wage growth and a closed output gap. Summary: The OECD projects the Bank of Japan will raise its short-term policy rate to 2% by the end of 2027, up from the current 0.75%, supported by higher inflation expectation...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.expand: The USDCHF continues to lean against its 100-hour moving average as a key resistance barometer. articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – AUD/USD, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran seemed to rise again, risk assets and stocks rose last week, while energy prices took a hit. Find out if a turnaround might happen this week as we face another Trump war deadline due Thursday.
